lint.details: 0 errors, 7 warnings (unused vars in error boundaries + gallery page)

nonBlockingIssues.description: localContext included verbatim in public questContext — minor privacy consideration since child could type identifying info
safeParseJSON utility duplicated in 6 files — should be extracted to src/lib/json-utils.ts
handleClusterClick uses .then() without .catch() — unhandled promise rejection possible
GeolocateControl requests viewer's browser location — design consideration for children's platform
Gallery page fetches entries twice on initial load (one for categories, one for map data)
AI description translation key exists but is never rendered in GalleryDetailClient
Flag dialog has no Escape key handler — accessibility gap
Flag endpoint has no rate limiting and only logs to console (data lost on restart)
entryId not sanitized before logging in flag endpoint
No component tests for Breadcrumbs or error boundary components
Session expiry lacks user-facing message — silent redirect to login
Many integration tests are static file-content checks rather than runtime tests
Discovery fetch error falls back to no-discovery state — could mislead users with network issues
Dead mock code in data persistence tests — mocks set up but route handlers never invoked

appliedUpdates.description: Added codebase-patterns.md documenting safeParseJSON duplication pattern, API field renaming convention (detectedTalents→talents), and gallery route placement outside (auth) group

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Extract shared utility functions (e.g., safeParseJSON) to src/lib/ rather than duplicating across route files. When a JSON parsing helper appears in 3+ files, it should be refactored to a shared module.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: safeParseJSON is identically duplicated in 6 files: gallery/entries/route.ts, gallery/entries/[id]/route.ts, gallery/entries/geojson/route.ts, quest/[id]/route.ts, quest/[id]/complete/route.ts, gallery/[id]/page.tsx. Multiple reviewers flagged this (gallery-entry-creation, gallery-world-map, cross-area-data-flow).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: API responses may rename Prisma schema fields for clarity (e.g., detectedTalents → talents). When renaming fields at the API boundary, document the mapping in a comment near the response construction.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-data-flow fixed a bug caused by field name mismatch — discovery API returned 'talents' but the quest page expected 'detectedTalents' (the Prisma field name). No convention existed for documenting these renames.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that containsSuspiciousPatterns() should not be exported or should have a JSDoc warning that it must not be used as a sanitization gate. The function is exported from src/lib/sanitize.ts but AGENTS.md explicitly prohibits using it to gate sanitization. Removing the export or adding a deprecation warning would prevent future misuse.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-user-journey observed that containsSuspiciousPatterns is exported and available for misuse despite AGENTS.md prohibiting its use as a gate. Tests verify routes don't use it, but the export is an accident waiting to happen.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update architecture.md: (1) Change middleware.ts references to proxy.ts (Next.js 16 convention), (2) Remove (auth) route group references since routes are at /[locale]/ directly with proxy-based auth gating, (3) Clarify gallery is outside auth group intentionally.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features cross-area-auth-and-navigation and gallery-world-map found architecture.md references middleware.ts (actual: proxy.ts) and shows gallery under (auth) route group (actual: outside it, publicly accessible via proxy publicPathPrefixes).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Fullstack-worker skill step 6 (agent-browser verification): Clarify that for API-only features without UI components, curl/API testing is an acceptable substitute for agent-browser verification. The current wording 'Invoke agent-browser to verify EACH user flow' is impractical for pure API features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features gallery-entry-creation and cross-area-data-flow workers used curl for API verification instead of agent-browser. Both produced valid verification but reported followedProcedure=true despite skipping agent-browser.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Fullstack-worker skill TDD procedure: Add guidance for fix/integration work where the primary task is fixing existing code rather than building new features. The strict 'red phase' (write failing test first) is hard to follow when the work involves understanding and fixing existing behavior across multiple files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature cross-area-data-flow worker feedback: 'TDD red phase instruction is hard to follow for cross-area integration fixes where the main work is fixing existing code rather than building new features.'

rejectedObservations.observation: Cross-area test file placement (src/app/api/__tests__/) is unconventional for tests spanning page components, translations, and layout concerns.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — there is no documented convention for cross-area test placement, and the current location works fine. Not worth establishing a convention for rare cross-area test files.

rejectedObservations.observation: Two features (cross-area-auth-and-navigation, gallery-ai-clustering) had completedWorkerSessionId='orchestrator-manual-timeout' with no handoff or transcript.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — this is a known orchestrator behavior when workers time out. The features' code was reviewed directly via git diff. No action needed on shared state.

rejectedObservations.observation: Gallery page unused useMemo import should be removed.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — already captured as a non-blocking issue. This is a minor cleanup, not a shared state observation. ESLint already flags it as a warning.
